methods.description: Process for analyzing protein secondary structure using ATR-FTIR spectra and curve fitting techniques.

steps.description: All spectra were treated with the OPUS software.
The analysis of the secondary structure of the proteins was carried out by curve fitting using the GRAMS AI software from Thermo Scientific.
steps.searchable_summary: perform curve fitting for protein secondary structure using GRAMS AI

steps.description: The second derivatives were calculated from the ATR-FTIR spectra after smoothing over nine consecutive points.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate second derivatives of spectra after smoothing

steps.description: A straight baseline passing through the ordinate at 1700 and 1610 cm -1 was subtracted before the curve fitting.
The baseline was again modified by the least-squares curve-fitting software, which allows for a horizontal baseline to be adjusted as an additional parameter to obtain the best fit.
The second derivative spectrum was used to determine the initial peak positions for curve fitting, and the peaks were fitted using Gauss functions.
steps.searchable_summary: fit peaks using Gauss functions based on second derivative spectrum

steps.description: The area under the entire band was considered as 100%, and each component after fitting was expressed as a percent fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percent fraction of each component after fitting

methods.description: Statistical tests and clustering analysis to evaluate differences among Betula species and analyze absorbance spectra.

steps.description: Firstly, the normal distribution and the homogeneity of variances were checked using Shapiro-Wilk and Brown-Forsyth tests, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: check normal distribution and homogeneity of variances

steps.description: The statistical hypothesis about the lack of differences among Betula species in respect to the diameter of pollen grains was tested using Kruskal-Wallis test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis test for differences among species

steps.description: Dunn's post hoc test was applied for multiple comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Dunn's post hoc test for multiple comparisons

steps.description: Hierarchical clustering analysis (HCA) with Euclidean distance and Ward's algorithms was used to indicate groups of species with highest similarity.
steps.searchable_summary: perform HCA using Euclidean distance and Ward's algorithms

steps.description: Raw data were standardized before calculating the Euclidean distance.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize raw data for Euclidean distance calculation

steps.description: Statistical and multidimensional analysis was performed using PAST software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical and multidimensional analysis using PAST
